mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.64% suggests room for further easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.3% is near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised upward to 1273.0 in Oct 2025
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with synchronized weakness across consumption and investment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over a 2-month window signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised downward to 101.4941 in Dec 2025
regime_hint: post-shock supply contraction or cyclical downturn
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 24026.834 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2025
regime_hint: above-trend expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals synchronized labor market deterioration that increases financial stress and reduces consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100k month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 158592 thousand as of Jan 2026
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3% year-over-year, the central bank maintains restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.64% and CPIAUCSL at 326.588 as of Jan 2026
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.3 million annualized units signal strong residential investment demand that supports construction employment and materials consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised to 1273.0 thousand units in Oct 2025
regime_hint: housing-led expansion with strong residential investment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1.3 million, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
